Off Calendar Mar-10-2026 Continued To Jun-23-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Chris Fargo-Masuda for petitioner Joseph Wu (via Zoom).
Steven Ellenberg for respondent Lydia Lee (via Zoom).
Jessica Neugebauer for respondent Gary Low (via Zoom).

The matter was called for hearing on 1) First Account and Report of Trustees and Petition for Approval; 2) First Account and Report of Attorney-In-Fact and Petition for Approval; and 3) Petition for Breach of Trust and Fiduciary Duties as Trustee, etc.

The Court inquired about the status of discovery and whether the matter was ready to be set for trial. Mr. Fargo-Masuda reported that petitioner had largely completed discovery and was requesting that the matter be set for trial. Mr. Ellenberg and Ms. Neugebauer reported that discovery was ongoing and that additional discovery remained, including depositions and responses to written discovery. They requested additional time before a trial date is set.

Mr. Fargo-Masuda stated that the petitions had been pending for a significant period of time and requested that a trial date be set to provide a target for completing the remaining discovery.

The Court addressed the parties regarding discovery and encouraged cooperation in completing discovery. The Court stated that it was premature to set a trial date and indicated that the matter would likely be referred to Department 206 for trial once discovery is complete.

The Court continued the matter to June 23, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Russell S. Roeca; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Petition (1) For Breach Of Trust And Fiduciary Duties As Trustee; (2) For Breach Of Fiduciary Duties As Attorney In Fact; (3) For Conversion; (4) For Fraud Or Deceit; (5) For Financial Abuse Of An Elder (Irene Low); (6) For Financial Abuse Of An Elder (Joseph Wu); (7) To Compel Return Of Trust Property And Or Estate Property; (8) To Quiet Title And Or For Reconveyance; And (9) For Imposition Of Constructive Trust
Off Calendar Feb-11-2025 Continued To Jun-03-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Steven A. Ellenberg for and with Lydia Lee, Respondent (via Zoom).
Jessica I. Neugebauer for Gary Low, Respondent (via Zoom).
Chris Fargo-Masuda for Joseph Wu, Petitioner (via Zoom).

Mr. Fargo-Masuda reported that trustee's counsel sent over an accounting, which is under review by his office. Initial review raised concerns regarding the accuracy and completeness of the financial information provided. Petitioner propounded first sets of discovery (interrogatories, requests for production) over seven months ago to both respondents. Some responses and document production have been received from Lydia Lee, but none have been received from Gary Low. Depositions have been noticed, scheduled, and re-scheduled multiple times.

The court suggested that the parties consider an informal discovery conference (IDC) to resolve disputes without filing a motion to compel. Petitioner's counsel expressed interest but noted that no date has been confirmed for outstanding discovery responses.

The court inquired whether the case was appropriate for mediation. Mr. Fargo-Masuda stated they were open to mediation but cannot proceed until discovery is complete.

The court continued the matter to June 3, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, to allow time for completion of discovery. The court ordered all parties to file a joint status report by May 27, 2025, regarding discovery and whether a Breslin order is needed.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)
